title: Add regression coverage for mandatory ticket status persistence and rules-template alignment
summary: Add regression coverage for the v0.4.11 contract so ticket status persistence remains explicitly mandatory across runtime guidance, versioned ticket artifacts, and the `.rules` template embedded in `zed-agentops-init.sh`. The tests should prevent future drift between the checked-in rules, generated rules template, and the expected persistence behavior for per-ticket JSON files plus `tickets_list.json`.
outputs: Regression tests for mandatory ticket status persistence
Regression tests for checked-in .rules and init-script template alignment
Verification evidence that status synchronization requirements remain enforced
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify that the checked-in `.rules` file and the `.rules` template embedded in `zed-agentops-init.sh` both require ticket status persistence to per-ticket JSON files and `tickets_list.json`.
Tests or contract checks verify that ticket status persistence is described as mandatory during execution, not optional bookkeeping.
Tests cover the expected synchronization rule between runtime transaction status and persisted ticket-document status strongly enough to prevent divergent interpretations.
Tests protect against drift between the checked-in `.rules` file and the generated init-script rules template for the relevant ticket-status language.
Repository verification passes with the new regression coverage in place.
tasks: Identify the existing test modules that should own rules-content and init-template alignment coverage.
Add or extend tests for mandatory ticket status persistence language in the checked-in `.rules` file.
Add or extend tests for matching ticket-status persistence language in the `zed-agentops-init.sh` embedded `.rules` template.
Add or extend contract checks that cover synchronization expectations between runtime ticket progress and persisted ticket-document status.
Run verification and confirm the v0.4.11 ticket-status persistence contract is covered by automated tests.
notes: Keep the tests focused on contract behavior and text alignment rather than incidental wording where possible.
Do not weaken strict workflow or transaction semantics in order to simplify the tests; the tests should reinforce the mandatory persistence contract.
Prefer assertions that lock the required persistence destinations and synchronization expectations directly.
Use this ticket to ensure future rule changes update both the checked-in `.rules` file and the init-script template together.
